other_observers: Jim Schmoker, other observers; Found by Cheyenne Laczek-Johnson and Nina Hill.
specific_location: S. of CR 64.5, about 300 meters E. of CR 91. Alternated between "main" pond to S. of road and secondary pond behind levy.
description: A white-plumaged, salmon-pink faced ibis. Curved ibis bill bright salmon-pink for the basal third or so, black the rest. Not sure how much of the dark part of the bill was possibly mud. Eye color pale blue. Legs and feet matching bright salmon-pink. Feathering all white except for black primary tips and a bit of pink-orange tinge to throat. Bird a bit larger than plegadis ibis nearby.Behaviors: Bird was feeding in shallow water and in wet grassy area. Sometimes it joined a flock of 40 or so plegadis ibis, sometimes stayed more by itself.Call: not heardPlumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker, Bill Schmoker
